description: Milky roadside tea, cardamom warmth, served in the spirit of a clay cup.
Black tea with lemon, salt, and the sharp lift of a rainy College Street evening.
Ginger-heavy tea for long conversations and monsoon throats.
Holy basil, slow milk, and a soft cream finish.
South Indian decoction, foamed high, strong enough for a second act.
Steeped overnight, mellow and dark, with a jaggery note.
A small café classic with cinnamon dust and a warm brown crema.
Crisp pastry, spiced potato, best eaten before the first argument cools.
Thick toast with salted butter, the old reliable companion to tea.
Seasonal fritters wrapped in paper-menu nostalgia.
Soft, dark, not too sweet, baked for late afternoon readers.
